today_mission.title: Turn comparison meanings into accurate sentences
today_mission.student_goal: Practice comparison structure control through the 'Comparison Meaning to Sentence' writing move.

items.rough_input: solar power / cheaper / coal (over the past decade)
wind energy / less reliable / solar power (in some regions)
nuclear power / more efficient / fossil fuels
renewable energy / faster growing / any other energy source (globally)

full_source_items.rough_input: solar power / cheaper / coal (over the past decade)
wind energy / less reliable / solar power (in some regions)
nuclear power / more efficient / fossil fuels
renewable energy / faster growing / any other energy source (globally)

steps: Write one accurate comparison sentence for each meaning prompt below.
Model (for reference only, do not copy word-for-word): Solar power has become cheaper than coal over the past decade.
Before you submit, check: Does the comparison structure match the intended meaning? Is the comparative or superlative form correct? Is the sentence grammatically complete?

required_output.length_guidance: 1 sentence per item unless the mission asks for a chain.

today_mission.student_instruction:
Turn comparison meanings into accurate sentences
Time: 10 minutes

What to do:
1. Write one accurate comparison sentence for each meaning prompt below.
2. Model (for reference only, do not copy word-for-word): Solar power has become cheaper than coal over the past decade.
3. Before you submit, check: Does the comparison structure match the intended meaning? Is the comparative or superlative form correct? Is the sentence grammatically complete?

Write your answer:
- 4 item(s)
- one sentence per line
- 1 sentence per item unless the mission asks for a chain.
success_checklist: Does the comparison structure match the intended meaning?
Is the comparative or superlative form correct?

observable_units.description: The comparative/superlative structure is grammatically correct.
The comparison matches the intended meaning of the prompt.
The sentence expresses a complete idea.
The sentence has a finite verb.
The learner produces the required number of items.

scoring.critical_failure_policy: critical observable-unit failure caps result at 0.55 unless evidence is partial and recoverable

outcomes.pass: score >= pass_threshold and no critical failure
outcomes.partial_pass: partial_threshold <= score < pass_threshold or minor recoverable gap

repeat_policy.if_fail: repeat same microskill with easier stimulus and heavier model support
repeat_policy.if_partial_pass: repeat same move family with one constraint reduced
repeat_policy.if_pass: repeat same microskill once in a new topic before unlocking dependent skill
repeat_policy.functional_rule: pass 2 of last 3 valid move missions with average >= 0.75 and no critical failure
repeat_policy.stable_rule: pass 4 of last 5 valid move missions with average >= 0.80 across at least 2 topics

next_steps: Complete today_mission only; do not rewrite the full essay.
